claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and does not live with you.
  - Charlie is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 18 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have three children - Alex who is 3, Blake who is 8, and Charlie who is 10. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! You have three children - Alex (3), Blake (8), and Charlie (10).

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for each of them, I need to ask you a few questions:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm based in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand your relationship with the children:

**Do all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, not all of them. Alex and Charlie live with me at home, but Blake doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. So Alex and Charlie live with you, but Blake doesn't.

**For Blake, who is 8 and doesn't live with you - are you paying towards Blake's upkeep (such as for food, clothes, or pocket money), and if so, how much do you contribute?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not currently contributing financially towards Blake's upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for any of these three children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Are any of your children currently in hospital, care, or residential accommodation for an extended period?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Actually, yes - Blake has been in hospital for a while now. It's been about 4 and a half months or so.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that. I'm sorry to hear Blake has been in hospital for so long.

Since Blake has been in hospital for about 4.5 months (which is over 12 weeks), this affects eligibility. However, there are exceptions to the rule.

**Are you regularly spending money on Blake while they're in hospital (for example, on clothes, toys, treats, or other items for them)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I am. I still buy clothes, magazines, and their favourite snacks and things like that for Blake while they're in there.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's very helpful.

Just one final question to complete my assessment:

**Are Alex and Charlie attending school/nursery as normal, and are they living with you full-time (not in any care arrangements or hospital)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. Alex and Charlie are both at home with me full-time and everything is normal - Alex goes to nursery and Charlie's at primary school.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Good News!

Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for all three of your children: Alex, Blake, and Charlie.**

Here's the breakdown for each child:

**Alex (age 3):** ✓ Eligible
- Alex lives with you in the UK and is under 16. You meet the standard eligibility requirements as you are responsible for Alex and live together.

**Blake (age 8):** ✓ Eligible
- Although Blake has been in hospital for over 12 weeks (about 4.5 months), you qualify for an important exception. Because you regularly spend money on Blake while they're in hospital (buying clothes, magazines, snacks, etc.), you can continue to receive Child Benefit for them even beyond the 12-week hospital stay limit.

**Charlie (age 10):** ✓ Eligible
- Charlie lives with you in the UK and is under 16. You meet the standard eligibility requirements as you are responsible for Charlie and live together.

Since nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of your children, you can claim for all three. If you haven't already applied, you can make a claim for Child Benefit. I hope Blake recovers soon and is able to come home!

reasonings: Alex is 3 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, and you are responsible for them. Alex lives with you full-time and attends nursery as normal. Under the standard Child Benefit rules, you qualify for Child Benefit for Alex as you are responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK together. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 8 years old (under 16) and you live in the UK. Although Blake does not live with you and has been in hospital for approximately 4.5 months (over the 12-week limit), you qualify for an exception to the standard rule. According to the rules, for children in a UK hospital, you can continue to receive Child Benefit after 12 weeks if you regularly spend money on them. You confirmed that you regularly buy clothes, magazines, snacks and other items for Blake while they are in hospital. This means you meet the exception criteria and remain eligible for Child Benefit for Blake. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 10 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, and you are responsible for them. Charlie lives with you full-time and attends primary school as normal. Under the standard Child Benefit rules, you qualify for Child Benefit for Charlie as you are responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK together. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) with three children: Alex (3), Blake (8), and Charlie (10). Alex and Charlie live at home full-time and attend nursery/primary school normally, so standard eligibility applies for both. Blake has been in hospital for approximately 4.5 months (over 12 weeks), which would normally affect eligibility, but the claimant regularly spends money on Blake (clothes, magazines, snacks), which qualifies them for the exception that allows Child Benefit to continue for children in UK hospitals beyond 12 weeks when the parent regularly spends money on them. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of the children. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for all three children.

Under the standard Child Benefit rules, you qualify for Child Benefit for Charlie as you are responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK together. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie.

Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of the children. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for all three children.
